deliverable_progress_summary:
**Programme Week 4** — strong momentum, critical blockers.

### Momentum
- **CLARA**: Executive endorsement from Cihan (MD Product & Tech), ~5,000 viewers imported
- **Build in Five**: Dashboard builder exceeded expectations — MCP server wiring is the critical gap
- **Salesforce**: Integration architecture decided (one-way, MCP middleware) but data mapping not started
- **Cross-OU**: Demand validated from Banking and Life Insurance

### Critical Risks
| Risk | Severity | Detail |
|------|----------|--------|
| BenVH/Nikhil conflict | **CRITICAL** (R#039) | At breaking point — Richard confrontation needed |
| Kathryn Palkovics COE threat | **HIGH** (R#045) | COE mandate overlaps AI programme |
| AWS costs | **MEDIUM** | $1,163 with zero attribution |

### Deliverable Concerns
- **D2 (Automated charters)** at 0% — target week 5 approaching
- **D7 (Salesforce integration)** — M18 data mapping target was this week, not started
- **Programme pace**: 6 of 38 milestones complete (16%) at 33% through timeline
programme_actions_summary:
### Week 4 Priorities

| # | Priority | Action | Origin |
|---|----------|--------|--------|
| 1 | **CRITICAL** | Resolve BenVH/Nikhil conflict — Richard confrontation + Ben Brookes escalation | Carried from Week 2 |
| 2 | **HIGH** | Secure Navigator MCP server access from Nicole for Martin | New — unlocks D10 + D11 |
| 3 | **HIGH** | Defend programme from Kathryn Palkovics COE absorption (Diana-Natalia + Richard-Diya channels) | Carried from Week 3 |
| 4 | MEDIUM | Begin Salesforce data mapping (M18 target this week) | New |
| 5 | MEDIUM | Begin adoption charter template design (D2 at 0%, target week 5) | New |
| 6 | MEDIUM | Execute cross-OU showcase with Idrees | Carried from Week 3 |
| 7 | LOW | Configure AWS Bedrock cost attribution tags | Carried from Week 3 |

actions.title: Review and finalize Gainsight Charter V1
actions.description: Azmain's charter needs stakeholder review. Must establish RACI before Kathryn Palkovics (COE) asserts further authority.
actions.context: **Gainsight Charter V1** was created by Azmain on 13 Mar and shared with the team. Ben Brookes immediately flagged business requirements that must be added: the integration cannot slow down migration progress or CLARA improvements, cannot lead to CSMs splitting between two systems, and cannot block adoption squads from accessing key info. Natalia Orzechowska suggested consulting CSMs on business requirements. Idrees Deen (Banking CS) offered help with Gainsight. Richard Dosoo pushed back hard — the team was not included in the scoping exercise that directly impacts their roadmap, and he wants a structured Phase 0 review of CLARA features with the Gainsight team present before proceeding.

actions.title: Complete Navigator MCP server integration for Build in Five
actions.description: Martin needs Navigator repo access from Nicole to wire up the MCP server, replacing the JavaScript agent stub with live API definitions. This completes the demo loop and is the single most impactful deliverable action this week. Unlocks D10 and D11.
actions.context:
**Navigator MCP server** is the integration layer that connects Martin's drag-drop dashboard builder to live API data. Currently the dashboard uses a JavaScript agent stub — replacing it with the Navigator MCP server means dashboards can pull real portfolio data dynamically.

This is critical because **AIG has signed a strategic deal with Anthropic and Palantir** and is using MCP servers to drive their entire underwriting agenda (revealed by Richard in the 11 Mar standup). Martin's Build in Five needs to demonstrate the same MCP-driven workflow transformation.

**Nicole** (Navigator team) controls repo access. Without it, Martin cannot wire up the server. BenVH expects the App Factory MCP server complete by end of week, making Martin the first integration POC.

actions.title: Begin adoption charter template design
actions.description: D2 has 0% progress. M4 (charter template designed) target is week 5 — must start this week. Multi-page documents with embedded images requiring OCR and multi-modal LLM. Rhett's uncoordinated Excel approach needs resolution. Carried theme from Week 1.
actions.context:
**Adoption charter** (Deliverable D2) is a structured template for onboarding customers onto IRP products. Currently at 0% progress with a target of week 5.

The Banking Credit team's **"Agent Day" framework** (presented by Wasim and Nils on 13 Mar) provides a model: they use a **four-tier adoption approach** — (1) Copilot Light, (2) Copilot Studio, (3) Cursor coding agents with MCPs, (4) Custom-built agents. Their key insight: **pre-work is everything** — surveys weeks before to identify use cases, all technical setup completed before the event.

Challenge: Rhett's uncoordinated Excel approach to adoption tracking needs resolution. Multi-page documents with embedded images require OCR and multi-modal LLM processing, which the current pipeline doesn't support.

actions.title: Defend programme independence from Catherine COE
actions.description: Carried from Week 3. Catherine's COE mandate overlaps AI programme. Organised Gainsight meeting without consulting team. Diana to discuss with Natalia Orzechowska. Richard to raise with Diya during London visit. Frame AI enablement as distinct from digital enablement. R#045.
actions.context:
**Catherine's Centre of Excellence (COE)** has three pillars: digital engagement, enablement, and Gainsight/SFDC retirement. The **first pillar (digital engagement) directly overlaps** with the AI programme's scope.

Key incidents (from 12 Mar Diana 1-1 and Post-Gainsight debrief):
- Catherine organised the Gainsight team meeting **without consulting the CLARA team**, blindsiding them
- She finds trivial CLARA issues every two days, claims CSMs complained (they haven't)
- She gatekeeps Gainsight/Salesforce access
- Her objectives were **approved by Natalia Orzechowska and signed off by Diya**

**Strategy**: Frame "digital enablement" (Catherine's domain) as distinct from "AI enablement" (Azmain's domain). Diana to discuss with Natalia O. Richard to raise with Diya during London visit. Risk R#045.

Richard to raise with Diya during London visit. Frame AI enablement as distinct from digital enablement. R#045.

actions.title: Plan Phase 0 CLARA feature review for Gainsight integration
actions.description: Charter V1 requires comprehensive review of existing CLARA features, data flows, and integration points before Gainsight work begins.
actions.context: **Richard Dosoo demanded a Phase 0 CLARA feature review** with the Gainsight team present before the integration proceeds further. His position: nobody from the CLARA team was involved in the initial assessment, meaning plans are being built on unvalidated assumptions. Specific gap identified: the adoption charter workflow does not appear in Gainsight documentation, yet CSMs are expected to produce significant charter volume. This review must happen before any commitments are made against functional gaps.

actions.title: Start Salesforce-IRP data mapping document
actions.description: M18 (data mapping documented) target is this week (week 4). Document which Cases/CaseFeed fields map to CLARA data. Coordinate with Kathryn Palkovics and Kevin Pern. Architecture decided in Week 3 — now execution.
actions.context:
**Salesforce-IRP data mapping** (Milestone M18) defines which Salesforce objects and fields map to CLARA's data model. The architecture decided on 11 Mar is **one-way only** (read from Salesforce, no write-back) using the **App Factory MCP server as middleware**.

Four user groups drive the requirements:
1. **Bernard** — customer sentiment analysis from real-time tickets
2. **Courtney Spillers** — HD adoption blockers from support cases (2,000-3,000 cases)
3. **Kevin Pern** — ticket info + possibly User Voice data
4. **Azmain/CLARA** — blocker intelligence synced from Salesforce

**Phase 1** focuses on Cases and Case Feed objects only. Kathryn Palkovics confirmed Gainsight goes live March 30 — CLARA and Gainsight are complementary (IRP-specific vs. overall CSM BAU).

actions.title: Chase Nicole for Navigator repo access
actions.description: Richard to follow up with Nicole urgently. Martin cannot proceed with MCP server wiring without repository access. Critical blocker for the exceedance demo.
actions.context:
**Nicole** is on the Navigator team and controls access to the Navigator repository. Martin cannot proceed with MCP server wiring without this access — it's the single biggest blocker for the Build in Five demo.

Richard committed to chasing Nicole in the 11 Mar standup. The Navigator MCP server would replace the current JavaScript agent stub with live API definitions, enabling Martin's dashboard builder to query real portfolio data. This is the critical path item for demonstrating workflow transformation at the exceedance demo.

actions.title: Prepare exceedance demo narrative with Ben Brooks
actions.description: Ben Brooks thinking about panel positioning. Schedule working session to define demo flow: customer problem, 18 months traditional, 5 minutes with Build in Five. AIG use case as anchor.
actions.context:
**Exceedance** is the upcoming demo event where Build in Five will be showcased. Ben Brooks saw Martin's dashboard builder for the first time on 10 Mar and was positive but strategically focused.

Ben's key framing question: **"Is this a Moody's product experience for customers to use inside the platform, or a self-service demonstration tool?"** — this fundamental product positioning must be resolved before the demo.

Ben wants to show a **common, irritating market workflow being solved in 5 minutes** — not just a cool dashboard, but a time transformation story. The demo format discussed: innovation track intro, customer challenges panel, then pivot to live/semi-live Build in Five demo. **Risk data lake** capabilities and portfolio context are potential additions.

actions.description: Ben Brookes thinking about panel positioning. Schedule working session to define demo flow: customer problem, 18 months traditional, 5 minutes with Build in Five. AIG use case as anchor.
actions.context:

Ben's key framing question: **"Is this a Moody's product experience for customers to use inside the platform, or a self-service demonstration tool?"** — this fundamental product positioning must be resolved before the demo.

actions.title: Document Banking agent day framework for reuse
actions.description: Capture Nils four-tier model, transit teacher concept, pre-work methodology, bi-weekly follow-ups. Adapt for insurance. Directly addresses stalled WS1.

actions.title: Document Banking agent day framework for WS1 reuse
actions.description: Capture Nils's four-tier model, transit teacher concept, pre-work methodology, bi-weekly follow-ups from Banking Credit team. Adapt for insurance. Directly addresses stalled WS1 Training & Enablement.
actions.context:
**Agent Days** are full-day in-person AI adoption events run by the Banking Credit team (Wasim and Nils). Their framework is directly relevant to the Training & Enablement deliverables (D4/D5).

**Four-tier agent model:**
1. **Copilot Light** — basic chat interface
2. **Copilot Studio** — low-code workflow builder
3. **Cursor coding agents with MCPs** — developer tools (target for most participants)
4. **Custom-built agents** — full autonomy

**Critical success factors:**
- **Pre-work** (surveys weeks before to identify use cases and experience levels)
- **"Transit teachers"** — 4+ trained multipliers pre-briefed on each participant's project
- All technical setup completed before the day
- Every participant builds their own agent (not group work) — everyone succeeded, including non-coders
- Shared GitHub repo of curated rules/skills with governance

actions.title: Plan CLARA viewer onboarding for 5,000 new users
actions.description: After importing ~5,000 viewers, create lightweight onboarding guide or FAQ. No helpdesk exists yet. Risk of support overwhelm if significant portion activates. R#049.
actions.context:
**Azmain announced on 13 Mar** that all ~4,000-5,000 people under Andy Frappe have been imported as CLARA viewers with SSO access. Anyone can log in to see everything; only certain people have edit access.

This was revealed at the CLARA demo to Cihan's entire Product & Technology team (~40-50 attendees). Cihan (MD Product & Technology) personally endorsed CLARA as a daily tool.

The risk (R#049, LOW): this massive viewer expansion happened **without a support plan**. No onboarding documentation, no FAQ, no feedback channel established yet. Azmain committed to setting up a feedback submission process, but the scale of potential questions from 5,000 new viewers could overwhelm the team — especially with only two grads arriving April 7.

actions.title: Deploy Friday PM to dev environment for pilot
actions.description: BenVH agreed to deploy Friday repo to dev without auth for 4-week pilot. Get the link, share with project managers, begin Diana's team pilot.
actions.context:
**Friday** is Azmain's programme management tool (named after the 1940 film "His Girl Friday"). Features: Kanban boards, list/timeline views, milestones, budgeting, decision tracking, stakeholder heatmaps, time tracking.

**Diana endorsed Friday** on 4 Mar: "today is the last Wednesday review meeting" — Friday replaces the slide-based Wednesday advisory project review. IRP projects sync bidirectionally with CLARA; non-IRP advisory projects live only in Friday.

**BenVH agreed to deploy** to dev environment without auth for a four-week pilot (confirmed 6 Mar). Azmain needs the link to share with project managers. **Prashant** allocated by Diana to help with development. This has been IN_PROGRESS since Week 2 — blocked on BenVH's bandwidth (he's also managing App Factory MCP server, Slidey deployment, and the Nikhil conflict).

actions.title: Organize Build in Five stakeholder share-out sessions
actions.description: Samuel Gibson added to group. Plan structured share-out for MPS, Sales, Product teams to manage growing interest.
actions.context: **Samuel Gibson** (Risk Modeller demo team, UK) was added to the Build in Five Teams chat by Ben Brookes on 16 Mar. Gibson is keen on MCP server possibilities for customer demos and wants to show capabilities to clients. Richard immediately flagged the need to connect Gibson and Mike with Martin's work, and asked Azmain to organize a stakeholder share-out — similar format to the App Factory showcase. Key stakeholders to include: MPS, Sales, and Product teams. Timing: once Martin's Build in Five work is ready for demonstration.

actions.title: Execute cross-OU showcase meeting (Wednesday)
actions.description: Richard and Idrees coordinating. Agenda: CS team AI updates across OUs, retention dashboard, ROB AI app, App Factory. ISLTR may be in London. Goal: build business case for dedicated team.

actions.title: Resolve BenVH/Nikhil conflict — direct confrontation
actions.description: Carried from Week 2. CRITICAL: BenVH stopped all communication with Nikhil. Richard planning (a) direct confrontation with Nikhil about boundaries, (b) escalation to Ben Brookes. Redirect Nikhil to Salesforce integration or AIG project. R#039. C#74, C#75.
actions.source_transcript_title: Chat with Rich - Diya s priorities
actions.context:
**BenVH/Nikhil conflict** has been escalating since Week 2 (Risk R#039, CRITICAL). In the 11 Mar App Factory standup, daily boundary violations were documented:

- Nikhil attempting to deploy without permission
- Introducing App Factory tasks to CAT team without authorisation
- Going directly to Richard about centralised logging after BenVH explicitly said it was out of scope
- Scheduling deployment meetings 30 minutes after being told no

BenVH explicitly stated he'd **"prefer to work overtime rather than have Nikhil interact with any App Factory app."** Richard is planning a direct confrontation with Nikhil about boundary violations and intends to escalate to Ben Brookes.

**Neutralisation strategy:** redirect Nikhil to Salesforce integration or AIG project, away from App Factory.

actions.title: Execute cross-OU showcase meeting
actions.description: Carried from Week 3. Richard and Idrees coordinating. Agenda: CS team AI updates across OUs, retention dashboard, ROB AI app, App Factory. Needs clear asks, not just demos. Goal: build business case for dedicated team. R#044.
actions.context:
**Cross-OU showcase** planned for Wednesday, deliberately timed because **ISLTR (Insurance Senior Leadership Team) is in London** — maximum visibility for cross-divisional AI traction.

Agenda items (discussed with Idrees on 11 Mar):
- CS team AI initiative updates across Operating Units
- Idrees's **segment-agnostic retention dashboard** (looped in DIA and George Dyke)
- **ROB AI app** for senior leadership visibility
- App Factory infrastructure demo

Idrees's strategic framing: showing cross-OU consistency helps DIA pressure Charlotte to move things forward. **AON intelligence:** Rob Fulber meeting Dan Dick (Global Head of Cat Risk at AON) next week in London — potential showcase opportunity.

actions.title: Resolve BenVH/Nikhil conflict
actions.description: BenVH stopped all communication with Nikhil. Richard planning direct confrontation and Ben Brooks escalation. Redirect Nikhil to Salesforce integration or AIG project. CRITICAL personnel risk.

actions.title: Establish App Factory governance framework with intake criteria
actions.description: Define gating criteria for App Factory intake and formalise Kathryn Palkovics' decision tree design. Merge the intake process, MCP architecture governance, and cross-OU adoption model into a single framework. Unblocks BenVH's queue.
actions.source_transcript_title: Portfolio review call discussion w Nat Ben
actions.context:
This action has been carried forward through **three consecutive weeks** without resolution — first raised in Week 1 as "Establish App Factory gating criteria" (id=40), then Week 2 as "Establish App Factory governance with Kathryn Palkovics intake design" (id=52), and Week 3 as "App Factory governance board with Kathryn Palkovics intake design" (id=70).

**Kathryn Palkovics** volunteered to design the decision tree on 6 Mar ("you're speaking my love language"). The 11 Mar App Factory Discussion established MCP server as central middleware. A Wednesday 12 Mar public launch meeting was planned with senior stakeholders.

BenVH has **4-5 apps queued** with zero governance. Duplicate apps being built independently (George's account planning, Idris's similar tool). Without formal intake criteria, every request gets built and BenVH's queue grows indefinitely.

actions.title: Resolve grad allocation confusion — Richard/Emma Monday meeting
actions.description: Richard and Emma to align on Alvin+Sam for CLARA (separate from Nikhil's Elliot replacement). Prevent Nikhil encroachment on CLARA resources.
actions.context: **Graduate allocation confusion** emerged on 16 Mar. Azmain requested 2 graduates (Alvin in London, Sam in New York) for AI programme work. Separately, Nikhil Koli asked Emma Jaggs for a replacement for Elliot (who left his team). Emma Jaggs confirmed only 2 grads total (Alvin + Sam), not 3. Richard Dosoo clarified to Emma that Azmain's request is completely separate from Nikhil's replacement need. Key risk: Nikhil may try to redirect one of the AI programme grads to backfill his own team shortage. Richard and Emma to meet Monday to resolve.

actions.title: Establish COE boundary: RACI for Gainsight workstream
actions.description: Kathryn Palkovics requested to join project team and asserted COE authority on charter. Need clear RACI to protect team autonomy.
actions.context: **Kathryn Palkovics (COE)** asserted authority over the Gainsight charter on 14 Mar, requesting to join the project team. This intersects with the Gainsight integration because the COE mandate overlaps with AI programme governance. A clear RACI is needed to define boundaries: who owns the Gainsight workstream decisions, who is consulted, and where COE authority ends. Without this, there is risk of dual governance causing delays and confusion.

actions.title: Configure AWS Bedrock cost attribution tags
actions.description: Carried from Week 3. $1,163 in first two weeks of March with zero attribution. Tags need configuring urgently. On pace for $10K/month. Everyone routing through one RMS Bedrock key.
actions.source_transcript_title: Chat with Rich about AWS Claude bedrock usage
actions.context:
**AWS Bedrock costs** reached $1,163 with zero attribution — nobody knows which apps or teams are consuming the tokens. Richard raised this on 10 Mar.

Multiple apps (CLARA, App Factory, Build in Five, Slidey) all use Claude via AWS Bedrock, but there are no cost attribution tags configured. Without tagging, the programme cannot demonstrate ROI per project or justify continued spend to Diya.

Azmain's personal token consumption was highlighted when he "ran through his monthly allowance in two days" building the 30-agent swarm for CLARA's reporting system.

actions.title: Cross-team Slidey collaboration meeting (Tuesday)
actions.description: Juliet, Richard, and others all building PowerPoint generators independently. Consolidate into one tool via App Factory.

actions.title: Slidey cross-team collaboration meeting
actions.description: Carried from Week 3. Juliet, Richard, and others all building PowerPoint generators independently. Tuesday meeting to consolidate into one tool via App Factory. R#041. C#76.
actions.context:
**Slidey** is Ben Brookes's presentation app — he built it as a personal tool and it gained traction internally. The Life Insurance team reacted strongly to it. Ben mentioned he "nearly didn't make it a Moody's app."

Slidey is now positioned as part of the App Factory platform alongside Martin's dashboard builder and CLARA. This meeting aims to coordinate across teams building similar tools to avoid duplication and ensure consistent App Factory architecture (MCP server middleware pattern).

BenVH completed Azure app registration for Slidey authentication on 11 Mar.

actions.title: Prepare for CLARA release on 27 March
actions.description: Fortnightly release cycle established by Natalia Plant. Next release 27 March. Chris to complete feedback pipeline fixes. Tuesday governance review.
actions.context:
**CLARA is transitioning from Build Mode to Maintenance Mode.** Natalia Plant called a meeting on 12 Mar to establish governance — she felt "lost" by the volume of recent changes.

New process established:
- **Fortnightly release cycle** with Tuesday review meetings for prioritisation
- Release schedule: skip next week, **release 27 March**, then grads take over
- **Two grads arriving April 7** (one London, one New York) — 100% dedicated to CLARA maintenance
- Chris maintaining feedback Excel: plan → build → test → push

Key items for 27 March release:
- **Analytics tab removal** from management dashboard (confusing for Diya)
- Management dashboard and Portfolio Review moved higher in sidebar
- Validation check: CSMs cannot mark use case "complete" if software status fields open
- Navigation UX fix: back button scroll position loss

actions.title: Set up Juliet Valencia AWS infrastructure
actions.description: Juliet needs separate AWS for commission data (too sensitive for shared hosting). Richard and BenVH to walkthrough setup.
actions.source_transcript_title: Chat with Rich - Diya s priorities
actions.context:
**Juliet Valencia** needs AWS infrastructure provisioned for her work. This was flagged in the 11 Mar App Factory standup as a tactical item.

The App Factory is expanding beyond the core team (BenVH, Martin, Nikhil) to include new contributors. AWS Bedrock access, IAM roles, and development environment setup are prerequisites. BenVH is pivoting the entire App Factory to an **MCP server architecture** — any new infrastructure should be configured to work within this middleware pattern rather than the old standalone app model.

Related: AWS costs are unattributed ($1,163 total) — new infrastructure should include cost attribution tags from the start.

actions.title: Close security audit exposure on personal LLM account usage
actions.description: Migrate all team members off personal Claude/Cursor accounts to AWS Bedrock. Document the transition for security team. Ensure no proprietary data is processed outside Moody's infrastructure going forward.
actions.context:
**Security audit caught the team using personal Claude accounts** for Moody's work in early March. Azmain admitted: "The security team caught it... we were just like, there's no proprietary information, which is wildly a lie." **Ben Brookes is providing political cover** but the risk persists.

The exposure: Azmain ran **30 concurrent agents** on Anthropic's cloud with full internet access — no enterprise controls, data outside Moody's infrastructure. Personal spend: £200/month combined (Azmain + Richard) from personal funds.

**AWS Bedrock API** is now working (Nikhil's contribution, 3 Mar) which should resolve the issue going forward. But the team needs to formally transition all usage and close out the historical exposure. Related to AWS cost attribution (action id=81) but distinct: this is about **security compliance**, not cost tracking.

snapshots.narrative: CLARA demoed to Cihan (MD Product & Tech). ~5,000 viewers imported. Blocker tracking now operational.
Charter template design not started. Target week 5. Grads arriving April 7 may free capacity.
CLARA replacing ad-hoc spreadsheets. Management dashboard in daily use by Cihan.
Onboarding stages live. Over 50% of migration target completed before Q1 ends.
No cross-product work yet. App Factory MCP architecture enables future support.
Salesforce integration design agreed. Implementation not started.
Salesforce MCP middleware decided. Data mapping not started.
Navigator API endpoints catalogued. MCP server integration is Build in Five top priority.
Design phase. Kevin Pern identified as Cases consumer.
Martin dashboard builder exceeded expectations. MCP server wiring is critical next step.
Ben Brooks engaged on exceedance positioning. AIG use case identified.
App Factory operational. Lucia team as first external customer. Chris ramping. Idris onboarded.
Banking and Life sessions held. Agent day framework discovered - ready-made model for WS1.

deliverable_progress_summary:
**Programme Week 1** — the formal starting gun.

### Key Developments
- **Diya endorsed** the three-pillar programme structure (23 Feb), confirming the 12-week resource plan
- **CLARA already live** — D1 milestone 1 complete from programme start
- **App Factory operational** under BenVH — D12 milestone 1 complete
- **Martin returned** from holiday and scoped three Build in Five demo scenarios for March 21 exceedance event

### Resource Commitment
| Person | Allocation |
|--------|-----------|
| Martin Davies | 75% |
| Chris | 50% |
| Nikhil | 50% |
| BenVH | 50% |
| Rotating grad | From April 7 |

### Emerging Risks
- **Token budget crisis** unresolved — $750 consumed in 3 days
- **Kathryn Palkovics** identified as active CLARA detractor
- **CLARA flat-pack concept** articulated: templatised deployment for cross-OU reuse
programme_actions_summary:
### Week 1 Outcome Summary

| # | Priority | Result |
|---|----------|--------|
| 1 | Secure Diya endorsement and 12-week resource commitment | **ACHIEVED** |
| 2 | Onboard Martin, Nikhil, and Chris | **ACHIEVED** |
| 3 | Define Build in Five demo scenarios | **ACHIEVED** |
| 4 | Resolve token budget crisis | **UNRESOLVED** — carries to Week 2 |
| 5 | Maintain CLARA feature freeze while clearing feedback backlog | **ONGOING** |

actions.title: Secure Diya endorsement of three-pillar programme structure
actions.description: Present three pillars (IRP Portfolio Governance, Platform-Embedded Intelligence, Internal Productivity) to Diya for executive backing. Outcome: endorsed with insurance IRP focus confirmed, no Life roadmap. D#28.

actions.title: Confirm 12-week resource plan with Diya
actions.description: Formalise resource commitment: Martin 75%, Chris 50%, Nikhil 50%, BenVH 50%. Rotating grad from April 7. Post-12-week requires business case for dedicated team of 3-5. D#29, C#44.

actions.title: Scope Build in Five demo scenarios for exceedance event
actions.description: Martin returned from holiday. Three scenarios defined: data integration service, custom analytics views, and webhook notification (Golden Bear lost deal). Foundation approach over scripted demo confirmed.

actions.title: Onboard Martin with programme docs and access
actions.description: Send all programme docs, set up Claude Code access, get Stacy sign-off on time allocation, create SharePoint folder, establish standup schedule. AI#147-158.

actions.title: Onboard Nikhil and Chris to programme
actions.description: AI infrastructure session on Feb 26 formally brought Nikhil and Chris in. Nikhil positioned on Cat Accelerate tech debt and App Factory. Chris on CLARA bug fixes. AI#158-168.

actions.title: Articulate CLARA flat-pack concept for cross-OU reuse
actions.description: Templatised architecture for independent deployment by other OUs, avoiding multi-tenant political triggers with Salesforce team. Emerged during Diya session planning.

actions.title: Maintain CLARA feature freeze and triage feedback backlog
actions.description: Feature freeze continued. Adoption charter deferred to sprint 2. Feedback management crisis: CSMs submitting trivial issues alongside genuine bugs. Severity governance needed.
actions.context:
**CLARA feature freeze** was mandated after Diya endorsed the three-pillar programme structure on 23 Feb. Scope is locked to **insurance IRP only** — no Life roadmap, no multi-tenant expansion.

The feedback backlog is significant: CSMs are treating CLARA like an enterprise product and submitting feature requests at scale. **Azmain** advocated for strict triage discipline — bugs get fixed, feature requests get logged but deferred. The **flat-pack model** (stripping out IRP-specific elements for cross-OU reuse) was proposed but is post-freeze work.

**Peter Kimes** submitted structured blocker feedback (User Voice URLs, JIRA IDs, Case URLs) but the team decided NOT to import User Voice data wholesale — links only, keeping User Voice as system of record. Reports feature has a breaking bug when adding a second object.

actions.title: Scope L1 ticket automation concept
actions.description: Agents managing Salesforce queue, answering via Navigator, routing or escalating. OpenClaw and Copilot Studio identified as agent frameworks. D8/D9 foundational work.

actions.title: Resolve token budget crisis
actions.description: Azmain burned $750 in 3 Cursor days. Claude Max expired. Team scrambling between Windsurf, OpenAI fallback, and Bedrock. R#018. No resolution this week — carries forward.
actions.context:
**Token budget crisis** hit on 23 Feb when Azmain burned **$750 in three Cursor days** (75% of monthly allowance). Claude Max subscription expired the same day, cutting off mid-build. The team scrambled to **Windsurf via Moody's SSO** as a free alternative.

Root cause: no enterprise AI tooling budget. Azmain and Richard are paying **£200/month combined from personal funds** for Claude Code subscriptions. Azmain described this as "insanely stupid" — the company should provide $200/month Claude subscriptions instead of $1,000/month Cursor licences.

**BenVH's Phantom Agent** was identified as the structural fix: dynamically provision isolated environments with cost controls per user/OU. Value proposition: one $5K dev laptop's cost can run Phantom Agent for 1,000 people for a month. Meanwhile, getting the **AWS Bedrock API key** working was the immediate tactical priority.

actions.title: Address Cat Accelerate tech debt exposure
actions.description: AI infrastructure session surfaced: no testing suite in 4 years, fragile CDK deployment, no non-prod environment. Rahul proposed prioritisation framework (value + risk + capacity).

actions.title: Build interim Salesforce data pipeline
actions.description: Sales Recon pilot disappointed: missing parent-child hierarchy, wrong dates, outdated SSO. Team decided to build own data access rather than wait for Sales Recon fixes.
actions.context:
**Sales Recon UAT failed** — missing parent-child hierarchy, wrong date queries, and outdated SSO SAML configuration. The team decided to **build an interim Salesforce/Mixpanel data pipeline** rather than wait for Sales Recon fixes.

This was discussed in the AI Infrastructure meeting on 26 Feb alongside broader tech debt concerns. **Rahul** proposed a structured prioritisation framework (value + risk mitigation + capacity). The immediate need: programmatic access to Salesforce data for CLARA's blocker intelligence.

**Stacy Dixstra** confirmed that monthly Excel exports from CLARA are sufficient for the migration dashboard in the interim — no APIs or automated feeds required yet. The scorecard target of 34 migrations is built on **fabricated dates** (CSMs made up switch-off dates in December), adding urgency to getting real data flowing.

actions.title: Establish App Factory gating criteria
actions.description: App Factory needs gating criteria for deployment decisions. Prevent unvetted apps from being deployed. Governance structure not yet designed — carries forward.
actions.context:
**App Factory** has **4-5 apps queued** for BenVH's deployment (Idris, Rhett, Stacy, Eddie) with **zero governance** — every request gets built, no product management discipline to filter.

Richard wasted an entire weekend rewriting Steve Gentilli's code, only to discover Steve was simultaneously updating the same app with Rhett — a coordination failure that highlights the lack of intake process.

**Azmain** designed deliberate friction into the GSD (Build in Five) intake as a governance mechanism disguised as UX: if the problem is trivial, structured questions discourage wasted build resources. But this only covers new builds — existing App Factory requests bypass it entirely.

BenVH is the **sole deployment bottleneck** — he manually deploys every app. Without gating criteria, his queue grows indefinitely. The Phantom Agent automation is the structural fix but isn't ready yet.

snapshots.narrative: CLARA dashboard live and operational since programme start. Feature freeze maintained. Flat-pack concept articulated for cross-OU reuse.
Charter work deferred to sprint 2. Template design target is week 5 — on schedule.
Baseline measurement target is week 2. Not yet started but on schedule.
Onboarding stages target week 3. IRP onboarding mapping underway.
Future phases — not yet scheduled. No action expected until week 8+.
Concept not yet scoped. Meeting brief template target week 5.
Sales Recon pilot disappointing. Own Salesforce data path being considered. Data mapping target week 4.
Navigator API analysis not yet started. Target week 3.
L1 automation scoped conceptually. Depends on Navigator API and Salesforce integration.
Martin returned from holiday. Three demo scenarios scoped. Framework documentation target week 2.
Demo environment setup not started. Foundation approach confirmed over scripted demo.
App Factory operational under BenVH. Four apps in pipeline. Gating criteria discussion started.
Cross-OU outreach not yet formalised. Maps team reversed from refusal to offering help after Diya backing.

deliverable_progress_summary:
**Programme Week 2** — fractures beneath operational progress.

### Personnel Crises
Three converging threats to programme stability:
- **BenVH burnout** — triggered by Nikhil appropriating his App Factory work (renaming it "Moplit")
- **Richard flight risk** — briefly exploring other positions
- **Azmain disengagement** — browsing internal job boards

### Operational Progress
- **Friday PM app launched** — built by 30 concurrent AI agents in one day
- **CLARA shown to Life SLT** with flat-pack interest
- **Kathryn Palkovics pivot** — emerged as surprise governance ally (detractor → facilitating Gainsight meeting)
- **Dan Flemington** brought Julia Valencia with Salesforce CDR access

### Milestones Completed
| Milestone | Deliverable | Significance |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| M7: Baseline manual effort measured | D3 | Quantified current reporting burden |
| M10: Onboarding stages defined | D4 | IRP onboarding stages mapped |
| M27: Build in Five framework documented | D10 | Martin onboarded, framework established |

actions.title: Launch Friday PM tool and secure Diana endorsement
actions.description: Azmain built Friday with 30 concurrent AI agents in one day (burning entire $1,000 monthly Cursor budget). Diana endorsed it as replacement for slide-based Wednesday advisory review. IRP projects sync bidirectionally with CLARA. D#34, D#35.

actions.title: Formalise Gainsight integration relationship via Catherine
actions.description: Catherine transformed from detractor to governance ally. Set up formal meeting with Tina Palumbo, Nadim, Rajesh. Volunteered for App Factory intake governance. CLARA positioned as complementary to Gainsight. D#37.

actions.title: Formalise Gainsight integration relationship via Kathryn Palkovics
actions.description: Kathryn Palkovics transformed from detractor to governance ally. Set up formal meeting with Tina Palumbo, Nadim, Rajesh. Volunteered for App Factory intake governance. CLARA positioned as complementary to Gainsight. D#37.

actions.title: Assign Chris to CLARA bug stabilisation
actions.description: Chris began working through consolidated feedback list. Discovered many early items already fixed but never documented — systemic feedback management gap. D#31.

actions.title: Get Nikhil Bedrock API key working
actions.description: Nikhil confirmed Bedrock Claude API working via OU-based IAM roles on his local environment. Significant technical milestone — resolves token budget crisis technically.

actions.title: Shift Build in Five demo timeline from March 21 to May
actions.description: Demo target moved to Exceedance conference in May, with content agreed by April. Martin to reverse-engineer Apollo dashboard into spec and rebuild. Modular UI with drag-and-drop IRP API modules. D#36.

actions.title: Redesign migration dashboard for Diya scorecard needs
actions.description: Session with Stacy and Natalia: removed confusing grey box, consolidated Hosting Plus under Risk Link, RBO under Risk Browser. New Scorecard tab with quarterly migration view. Credibility risk: 4 pre-2026 completions counted in 2026. R#028.

actions.title: Open Salesforce data path via Dan Flemington
actions.description: Dan brought working Cursor-built tools and Julia Valencia with SFDC CDR access. His variance report concept adopted for CLARA 'what changed since last login' view. Convergence: Bernard, Courtney, Kevin Pern, Dan all need Salesforce programmatic access.

actions.title: Maintain CLARA feature freeze and triage feedback
actions.description: Carried from Week 1. Feature freeze maintained. Two-week release cycle adopted (Friday releases). 113 git migration heads squashed to one. Feedback triage improving with Chris.

actions.title: Address BenVH burnout and Nikhil credit-taking crisis
actions.description: BenVH admitted absence was not illness — Nikhil renaming App Factory to 'Moplit', presenting architecture as his own, receiving public credit while BenVH unmentioned. Explicit parallel to traumatic Microsoft experience. R#024. CRITICAL personnel risk — not resolved this week.
actions.context:
**BenVH's absence was NOT illness** — he was worn down by Nikhil taking credit for App Factory work. This explicitly parallels his **traumatic Microsoft experience**: a manager took his ideas, put their name on them, and destroyed his career.

Specific grievances: Nikhil sending BenVH's architecture diagrams as his own, renaming App Factory to **"Moplit"**, speaking in advisory all-hands about App Factory without crediting BenVH, and trying to overwrite Cat Accelerate processes.

BenVH is emotionally fragile and considering apartment relocation (financial stress). **All three key technical people** were simultaneously considering exit: Richard briefly explored other positions (resolved mid-March), Azmain was looking at internal Moody's jobs, and BenVH was at breaking point.

**Response plan**: Azmain committed to name-dropping App Factory and Phantom Agent in group chats to establish credit. Both planning separate conversations with Richard about addressing Nikhil. Strategy: redirect Nikhil to client-facing IRP work.

actions.title: Resolve token budget and security audit exposure
actions.description: Carried from Week 1. Evolved from budget problem to security audit: caught using personal Claude for proprietary data. Azmain admitted 'no proprietary info' defence is false. Ben Brookes providing cover. Bedrock migration resolves going forward. R#018 → R#026.
actions.context:
**Security audit caught the team using personal Claude accounts** for Moody's work. Azmain admitted to Chris: "The security team caught it... we were just like, there's no proprietary information, which is wildly a lie, but whatever. Ben is providing cover for us."

The exposure is significant: Azmain ran **30 concurrent agents** in Cursor on Anthropic's cloud infrastructure with full internet access — no enterprise controls, data processing outside Moody's infrastructure. Personal spend: Azmain burned his entire **$1,000 monthly Cursor budget in ONE DAY** building Friday.

**Ben Brookes is providing political cover** but the risk persists if re-examined formally. The **AWS Bedrock API** (working since Nikhil's contribution on 3 Mar) should resolve the issue going forward by keeping data within Moody's infrastructure, but the historical exposure remains unaddressed.

actions.title: Establish App Factory governance with Kathryn Palkovics intake design
actions.description: Carried from Week 1. Kathryn Palkovics volunteered to design decision tree for App Factory intake — first external stakeholder offering governance help. Partly to prevent Rhett deploying unvetted apps.
actions.context:
**Kathryn Palkovics** volunteered to design an **App Factory decision tree/intake process** during the Pre-Gainsight team meeting on 6 Mar. When the governance need was described, she responded: **"you're speaking my love language."**

Her proposed role: serve as a gating checkpoint — should an idea be built as a custom app, or does it already exist in Gainsight? This prevents silo creation. Examples: **George** building an account planning app and **Idris** (Banking) building something similar, both independently with no coordination.

Catherine sits between business and enterprise tooling, making her ideal for intake triage. She identified herself as a willing governance ally, NOT a blocker. However, Richard flagged that Gainsight integration is **NOT in the original 12-week plan** — replanning required if integration is expected.

actions.title: Establish App Factory governance with Catherine intake design
actions.description: Carried from Week 1. Catherine volunteered to design decision tree for App Factory intake — first external stakeholder offering governance help. Partly to prevent Rhett deploying unvetted apps.

Examples: **George** building an account planning app and **Idris** (Banking) building something similar, both independently with no coordination.

Catherine sits between business and enterprise tooling, making her ideal for intake triage. She identified herself as a willing governance ally, NOT a blocker. actions.title: Navigate Rhett adoption charter friction
actions.description: Rhett built Excel-based charter approach without consulting CSMs, contradicting agreed Word-to-app plan. Richard spent full day integrating his incomplete code. Political: 'Don't escalate to Ben Brookes — Rhett is Ben's favourite.'
actions.context:
**Rhett created an Excel-based adoption charter** without consulting CSMs, directly contradicting the agreed Word-to-app approach. The adoption charters turned out to be far more complex than expected — actual documents have **embedded images, diagrams, and multi-page formatting** requiring OCR and multi-modal LLM processing.

Richard spent a **full day reverse-engineering** Rhett's incomplete spec (5 Mar Clara standup). His explicit advice: **do NOT escalate to Ben Brookes** — Rhett has Ben's political protection.

Azmain set the priority sequence: bugs first → Bedrock blocker intelligence second → adoption charters third. The one-directional data flow decision (CLARA → document, not bi-directional) was made to limit scope. Meanwhile, Rhett was observed talking about App Factory in advisory meetings **without understanding it**, compounding BenVH's credit-taking frustration.

actions.title: Deploy Friday to dev environment for pilot
actions.description: BenVH agreed to deploy Friday to dev without authentication for a four-week pilot. Diana allocated Prashant as resource. C#65.
actions.context:
**Friday** is Azmain's programme management tool, named after the 1940 film "His Girl Friday." Features include Kanban boards, list views, timeline views, milestones, budgeting, decision tracking, stakeholder heatmaps, and time tracking.

**Diana explicitly endorsed Friday** in her 4 Mar 1-1: "today is the last Wednesday review meeting" — Friday replaces the slide-based Wednesday advisory project review. IRP projects sync bidirectionally with CLARA; non-IRP advisory projects (internal, ILS, cat bonds) live only in Friday.

**BenVH agreed to deploy** the Friday repo to dev environment without authentication for a four-week pilot (confirmed 6 Mar). Azmain needs the link to share with project managers. **Prashant** was allocated by Diana to help with Friday development. Azmain explicitly did NOT want Vlad ("launches into tangents about Natalia being awful").

deliverable_progress_summary:
**Programme Week 3** — the expansion week (23 transcripts, highest volume yet).

### Cross-OU Demand Validated
- **Banking**: Gina Greer, Olivier arrived asking for what Insurance has built
- **Life Insurance**: Jack Cheyne, Christian Curran expressing interest
- Both divisions want CLARA-equivalent capability

### Build in Five Breakthrough
- Martin's **dashboard builder exceeded expectations** — Richard compared it to Databricks Genie
- MCP server wiring identified as the critical gap to unlock full potential

### Architecture Decisions
- **App Factory** pivoted to MCP architecture (Phantom Agent positioning)
- **Salesforce integration** design crystallised: one-way read, Cases/CaseFeed, MCP middleware
- **Cihan** (MD Product & Tech) gave full CLARA endorsement after demo
- **~5,000 viewers** imported into CLARA

### Milestones Completed
| Milestone | Deliverable | Significance |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| M21: Navigator API endpoints catalogued | D8 | API analysis completed |
| M36: Cross-OU meeting cadence established | D13 | Banking + Life engagement formalised |

### Political Risk
- **Kathryn Palkovics' trajectory reversed again**: from ally back to confirmed COE political threat (R#045)

actions.title: Resolve BenVH/Nikhil conflict — now CRITICAL
actions.description: Carried from Week 2. Escalated to CRITICAL: BenVH stopped all communication with Nikhil, cancelled meetings. Daily boundary violations: scheduling meetings after being told no, introducing tasks to CAT team, going around BenVH to Richard. Richard planning direct confrontation and Ben Brookes escalation. R#039. C#74, C#75.
actions.source_transcript_title: Chat with Rich - Diya s priorities
actions.context:
The **BenVH/Nikhil conflict** escalated to CRITICAL in week 3. In the 11 Mar App Factory standup, daily boundary violations were documented:

- Nikhil attempting to deploy without permission
- Introducing App Factory tasks to CAT team without authorisation
- Going directly to Richard about centralised logging after BenVH explicitly said it was out of scope
- Scheduling deployment meetings 30 minutes after being told no

BenVH explicitly stated he'd **"prefer to work overtime rather than have Nikhil interact with any App Factory app."** Richard is planning a direct confrontation with Nikhil about boundary violations and intends to escalate to Ben Brookes.

**Neutralisation strategy:** redirect Nikhil to Salesforce integration or AIG project, away from App Factory.

actions.title: Manage Kathryn Palkovics COE political threat to programme
actions.description: Kathryn Palkovics' COE mandate overlaps AI programme. Organised Gainsight meeting without consulting CLARA team. Positioning to absorb programme under her remit. Diana to discuss with Natalia O. Richard to raise with Diya. R#045.
actions.context:
**Kathryn Palkovics' Centre of Excellence (COE)** has three pillars: digital engagement, enablement, and Gainsight/SFDC retirement. The **first pillar (digital engagement) directly overlaps** with the AI programme's scope.

Key incidents (from 12 Mar Diana 1-1 and Post-Gainsight debrief):
- Kathryn Palkovics organised the Gainsight team meeting **without consulting the CLARA team**, blindsiding them
- She finds trivial CLARA issues every two days, claims CSMs complained (they haven't)
- She gatekeeps Gainsight/Salesforce access
- Her objectives were **approved by Natalia Orzechowska and signed off by Diya**

**Strategy**: Frame "digital enablement" (Kathryn Palkovics' domain) as distinct from "AI enablement" (Azmain's domain). Diana to discuss with Natalia O. Richard to raise with Diya during London visit. Risk R#045.

actions.title: Configure AWS Bedrock cost attribution tags
actions.description: $1,163 in first two weeks of March with zero attribution. Everyone routing through one RMS Bedrock key. Tags need configuring urgently. On pace for $10K/month.
actions.source_transcript_title: Chat with Rich about AWS Claude bedrock usage
actions.context:
**AWS Bedrock costs** reached **$1,163 with zero attribution** — nobody knows which apps or teams are consuming the tokens. Richard raised this on 10 Mar.

Multiple apps (CLARA, App Factory, Build in Five, Slidey) all use Claude via AWS Bedrock, but there are **no cost attribution tags** configured. Without tagging, the programme cannot demonstrate ROI per project or justify continued spend to Diya.

Azmain's personal token consumption was highlighted when he "ran through his monthly allowance in two days" building the 30-agent swarm for CLARA's reporting system. Cost attribution is a prerequisite for scaling Bedrock usage across the programme.

actions.title: Plan cross-OU showcase meeting for Week 4
actions.description: Richard and Idrees coordinating cross-OU showcase. Agenda: CS team AI updates, retention dashboard, ROB AI app, App Factory. Risk of becoming demo-fest without clear asks. R#044. C#73-87.
actions.context:
**Cross-OU showcase** planned for Wednesday, deliberately timed because **ISLTR (Insurance Senior Leadership Team) is in London** — maximum visibility for cross-divisional AI traction.

Agenda items (discussed with Idrees on 11 Mar):
- CS team AI initiative updates across Operating Units
- Idrees's **segment-agnostic retention dashboard** (looped in DIA and George Dyke)
- **ROB AI app** for senior leadership visibility
- App Factory infrastructure demo

Idrees's strategic framing: showing cross-OU consistency helps DIA pressure Charlotte to move things forward. **AON intelligence:** Rob Fulber meeting Dan Dick (Global Head of Cat Risk at AON) next week in London — potential showcase opportunity.

actions.title: Plan Slidey cross-team collaboration meeting
actions.description: Juliet, Richard, and others all building PowerPoint generators independently. Need to consolidate into one tool via App Factory. Meeting planned for Tuesday Week 4. R#041. C#76.
actions.source_transcript_title: Next steps on dev work w RIch
actions.context:
**Slidey convergence** was discovered on 11 Mar: **Juliet Valencia** (Sales) independently built a Moody's-branded PowerPoint generator — the same tool Richard's team is building as **Slidey** (Ben Brookes's presentation app).

Juliet's bot connects to Databricks but can't write to SharePoint in the Azure sandbox. She opted for her **own AWS infrastructure** rather than shared App Factory (data governance: commission targets are confidential).

**Azmain's insight:** "Of all the complicated, fancy stuff we do, this will be the one that reaches the most people." Richard to schedule a cross-team Slidey meeting on Tuesday. Juliet to share her GitHub repo. App-to-app communication (CLARA → Slidey: write MD file, auto-generate PowerPoint) is a key integration use case.

actions.title: App Factory governance board with Catherine intake design
actions.description: Carried from Weeks 1-2. Catherine actively designing decision tree. MCP pivot adds architectural clarity. Governance now includes: intake criteria, cost controls, deployment standards.
actions.source_transcript_title: Portfolio review call discussion w Nat Ben
actions.context:
**App Factory governance** discussed at the 11 Mar App Factory Discussion meeting. The core architecture decision: **MCP server is the central middleware** — all apps (CLARA, Slidey, Build in Five) connect to a central App Factory MCP server instead of building per-app LLM pipelines.

**Wednesday 12 Mar** was set as the public launch meeting with senior stakeholders (Ben Brooks, George, CS contacts). Catherine's intake design from week 2 feeds into this governance board.

Key decisions: no more per-app LLM pipelines, SharePoint knowledge cataloguing initiative (profile 10 years of consulting documents, vector DB, MCP on top for RAG). **BenVH** to complete App Factory MCP server for Martin integration by 15 Mar. Cross-OU adoption model being developed — insurance team sharing infrastructure with Banking and Life Insurance.
